title: thymus serpyllum var. glabrescens
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. glabrescens, also known as creeping thyme, is a type of flowering plant native to Europe, with small, pink to purple flowers and a creeping habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum var. glabrescens have been shown to provide various health benefits, including relief from coughs and colds, improved digestion, and reduced anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in potpourri and perfumes, due to its pleasant fragrance and potential medicinal uses.
